_comment: HAND-BUILT v2 findings fixture (Build 17). House sweep — the wider aimed board: partnership/BD, threat/financing, blind-spot detection, discovery. FACTS + sources + coverage_notes only; house_lens is the shallow two-lens tag the contract allows, NOT a read_through. No read_through/thesis_bearing/priority — the manager's. Facts grounded in docs/schema/sample-issue-hmbd-001-2026-07-18.json and docs/research/program-detective-source-set-2026.md.

findings.summary: Merck & Co. (MSD) owns HER3-DXd through its 2023 Daiichi Sankyo ADC license. Separately, cetuximab (marketed ex-US by Merck KGaA, a distinct corporate Merck) is the combination agent in HMBD-001's Phase 1b squamous arm. The two 'Merck' entities are frequently conflated.

Daiichi Sankyo's DXd antibody-drug-conjugate platform is the modality engine behind HER3-DXd and a broad ADC franchise across multiple targets. The platform can be re-aimed at whichever HER3 setting reads out first — a company-unit exposure rather than a single-asset one.
A preclinical anti-HER3 CAR-T candidate was disclosed in an AACR conference poster this cycle — the earliest-stage HER3 competitor surfaced. Poster-stage only; no clinical trial registered.

proposed_entity.name: An anti-HER3 CAR-T candidate (preclinical)
proposed_entity.what_it_is: Preclinical anti-HER3 CAR-T disclosed in an AACR poster; a third distinct HER3 modality after ADC and bispecific ADC.

findings.summary: Blind-spot flag: China-first HER3 and squamous assets (SDP0505 via CDE/chictr; Akeso/ivonescimab financing via HKEX 9926.HK) have no clean free feed and are language-gated — the assets the free-feed detective sees latest, and the closest modality-adjacent and setting rivals to HMBD-001.

coverage_notes.notes: Two lenses on one sweep: the Merck entanglement reads under partnership/BD, the DXd platform under threat/financing. One preclinical discovery (HER3 CAR-T) proposed. China-first registry/financing remains the rank-1 named blind spot; analyst/broker interpretation is paywalled and out of scope for the decision-owner reader.
